function.name: add_scene_node
function.description: Add or update a node (object, room, surface, region, or agent) in the polaroid scene graph. Use this when you observe a new entity in the environment.

label.description: Human-readable identifier for the node, e.g. 'door-1', 'table-A', 'room-kitchen'.

properties.description: Key-value pairs describing the node, e.g. {"color": "brown", "state": "open"}.

agent_id.description: ID of the agent that observed this node.

db.description: Path to the SQLite scene database. Defaults to .polaroid/scene.db.

function.name: query_nodes
function.description: Query nodes in the polaroid scene graph by type, label substring, or minimum confidence. Use this to find objects, rooms, or surfaces in the environment.

node_type.description: Filter by node type: object, room, surface, region, or agent.

label_contains.description: Case-insensitive substring to match against node labels.

db.description: Path to the SQLite scene database. Defaults to .polaroid/scene.db.

function.name: get_scene_context
function.description: Get a human-readable text summary of the current scene graph. Returns counts by type, known object labels, and edge count. Use this to give an LLM situational awareness about the environment.

agent_id.description: If provided, only include nodes observed by this agent.

db.description: Path to the SQLite scene database. Defaults to .polaroid/scene.db.

function.name: add_scene_edge
function.description: Add a directed spatial relationship (edge) between two nodes. Use this to record that one entity contains, is adjacent to, or is on top of another.

source_id.description: ID of the source node (16-char hex content-addressed ID).

target_id.description: ID of the target node (16-char hex content-addressed ID).

relation.description: The spatial relationship from source to target.

db.description: Path to the SQLite scene database. Defaults to .polaroid/scene.db.
